papers.title: Achieving the Defect Transfer Detection of Semiconductor Wafer by a Novel Prototype Learning-Based Semantic Segmentation Network
papers.abstract: Recently, deep-learning-based methods have achieved excellent performance in semiconductor wafer defect inspection. However, these trained networks are only effective for a certain background pattern and fail in defect transfer detection between multiple background patterns. Once the background pattern has changed, images are needed to be recollected and relabeled, which is very time-consuming. To achieve defect transfer detection in semiconductor wafers, a novel prototype learning (PL)-based ne
papers.venue: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.why_selected: Prototype learning 기반으로 다양한 배경 패턴 간 defect transfer detection을 수행하며, 배경 변화에 대한 강건성과 재학습 없이 새로운 패턴에 적응하는 문제를 다루어 본 연구의 style/noise 변화 강건성 요구사항과 직접 관련됨.

papers.title: Attention Modules Improve Image-Level Anomaly Detection for Industrial Inspection: A DifferNet Case Study
papers.abstract: Within (semi-)automated visual industrial inspection, learning-based approaches for assessing visual defects, including deep neural networks, enable the processing of otherwise small defect patterns in pixel size on high-resolution imagery. The emergence of these often rarely occurring defect patterns explains the general need for labeled data corpora. To alleviate this issue and advance the current state of the art in unsupervised visual inspection, this work proposes a DifferNet-based solution
papers.venue: IEEE Workshop/Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision
papers.why_selected: DifferNet 기반 비지도 이상 탐지에 attention 모듈을 추가하여 고해상도 산업 이미지에서 미세 결함 패턴을 감지하는 메커니즘을 제안하며, normal-only 학습 기반 anomaly detection의 baseline 역할을 할 수 있음.

papers.title: Defect detection techniques robust to process variation in semiconductor inspection
papers.abstract: The downscaling of device dimensions in semiconductor manufacturing has meant that critical defect sizes have become smaller and smaller. This makes it more likely that the highly sensitive optical wafer inspection tool used for detecting small defects will erroneously detect process variations as defects, and generate a large amount of ‘nuisance’ information. Therefore, the scanning electron microscope (SEM)-based review tool used needs to automatically discriminate between defects and nuisance
papers.why_selected: 반도체 공정 변이(process variation)로 인한 nuisance/false positive 문제를 다루며, 본 연구에서 요구하는 공정 노이즈 및 스타일 변화에 따른 false positive 억제 제약 조건과 직접적으로 관련됨.

papers.title: Simulation of semiconductor wafer dicing induced faults on chips and their application as augmentation method for a deep learning based visual inspection system
papers.abstract: In semiconductor wafer dicing, one particular area of interest is the process of visual inspection to detect manufacturing defects that occur throughout the manufacturing process. Emerging defect patterns are typically in the micrometer range, translating to barely visible defects in pixel size on high-resolution imagery. The availability of rarely occurring defects is generally limited due to the labor-intensive nature of the related, highly specialized annotation task. Therefore, this contribu
papers.why_selected: 반도체 웨이퍼 다이싱에서 고해상도 이미지의 미세 결함 탐지 및 희소 결함 데이터 문제를 시뮬레이션 기반 augmentation으로 해결하려는 접근으로, 결함 데이터 부족 제약 조건의 배경 근거를 제공함.

papers.title: A Novel Gan-Based Data Augmentation Algorithm for Semiconductor Defect Inspection
papers.abstract: A deep learning solution is proposed for the problem of object inspection in semiconductor images. Supervised learning method approaches require large annotated semiconductor datasets, which are often difficult to obtain. Therefore, we develop a new deep convolutional generative adversarial network (DCGAN)) to generate simulated data. Real image data and generated image data are used to train the residual network (ResNet) defect inspection network. Compared to training with the original dataset,
papers.why_selected: GAN 기반 데이터 증강으로 반도체 결함 데이터 부족 문제를 해결하는 지도학습 접근법으로, 본 연구가 지도학습 대안(normal-only)을 추구하는 이유의 비교 기준이 됨.

papers.title: Anomaly detection with convolutional neural networks for industrial surface inspection
papers.abstract: Abstract Over the recent years Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) have become the primary choice for many image-processing problems. Regarding industrial applications, they are hence especially interesting for automated optical quality inspection. However, with well-optimized processes is it often not possible to obtain a sufficiently large set of defective samples for CNN-based classification and the training objective shifts from defect classification to anomaly detection. Here we approach th
papers.why_selected: CNN 기반 산업 표면 검사에서 정상 샘플만으로 anomaly detection을 수행하는 비지도 접근법을 다루어, normal-only reference 기반 결함 탐지 시스템의 핵심 선행 연구에 해당함.

papers.title: Machine learning based on using layout to generate reference SEM images for defect inspection
papers.abstract: In semiconductor manufacturing, the detection of defects efficiently and accurately plays an important role in improving production quality and process optimization. However, most of the current defect inspection methods need to collect reference images on wafer. Based on the machine learning (ML) model, this paper first using the layout to generate the corresponding Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) image as the reference image for defect, and then by comparing the similarity of the defect ima
papers.why_selected: Layout에서 reference SEM 이미지를 생성하여 결함 검출에 활용하는 방법으로, 웨이퍼 상에서 직접 reference를 수집하지 않고 참조 이미지 기반 비교 검사를 수행하는 본 연구의 핵심 접근과 가장 유사함.

papers.title: Addressing Class Imbalance and Data Limitations in Advanced Node Semiconductor Defect Inspection: A Generative Approach for SEM Images
papers.abstract: As semiconductor technology advances in accordance with Moore's Law, the continuous reduction in feature sizes presents an escalating challenge in defect detection. The limitations imposed by optical and e-beam technologies further contribute to the complexity of identifying defects at these shrinking scales, owing to increasing noise and decreasing image contrast levels. Precision in identifying nanometer-scale device- killer defects is crucial in both semiconductor research and development as

papers.why_selected: Sections의 tables에서 DDPM 기반 합성 데이터로 YOLOv5/v8 학습 성능을 보고하며, 반도체 SEM 이미지의 class imbalance와 데이터 부족 문제를 다루는 지도학습 baseline으로 활용 가능함.

papers.title: A Novel Pixel-Wise Defect Inspection Method Based on Stable Background Reconstruction
papers.abstract: In this article, an anomaly detection method based on background reconstruction is proposed to perform defect inspection on the texture surface of the industrial products. This method consists of two modules: 1) an autoencoder integrated with a generative adversarial network is utilized to reconstruct the textured background of the original image as a defect-free reference. Specifically, extra anomalous images are introduced and a mapping method of anomaly is given to improve the stability of re
papers.venue: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.why_selected: 배경 재구성(autoencoder+GAN) 기반으로 defect-free reference를 생성한 후 원본과 비교하여 픽셀 단위 결함을 탐지하는 방법으로, normal reference 기반 anomaly map 생성이라는 본 연구의 핵심 메커니즘과 직접 관련됨.

papers.title: GAN-based Defect Synthesis for Anomaly Detection in Fabrics
papers.abstract: Image-based quality control aims at detecting anomalies in products and is a crucial part of the production process. Challenges arise from the complexity and variety of products, defects, and the rarity of defect occurrence. Quality control thus still relies heavily on manual inspection. Supervised, data driven approaches have greatly improved performance, but suffer from a major drawback: They require large amounts of annotated training data, limiting their economic viability.In this work, we o
papers.venue: IEEE International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Factory Automation
papers.why_selected: GAN 기반 결함 합성으로 anomaly detection 성능을 향상시키는 접근으로, 결함 데이터 희소성 제약 조건 하에서의 대안적 메커니즘을 보여주는 참고 자료임.

papers.title: Automated Optical Inspection Using Anomaly Detection and Unsupervised Defect Clustering
papers.abstract: Neural networks have proven to be extraordinarily successful in many computer vision applications. But the approaches used to train neural networks require large datasets of annotated images, which requires a solid amount of human time to prepare those datasets. To facilitate the adoption of machine learning based technologies in industrial computer vision applications, this paper presents a two-step unsupervised learning approach for anomaly detection with further defect clusterization. In the
papers.venue: IEEE International Conference on Emerging Technologies and Factory Automation
papers.why_selected: 비지도 anomaly detection 후 결함 클러스터링까지 수행하는 2단계 파이프라인으로, 라벨 없이 결함 후보를 검출하고 분류하는 본 연구와 유사한 비지도 검사 baseline을 제공함.

papers.title: Scanning electron microscopy-based automatic defect inspection for semiconductor manufacturing: a systematic review
papers.abstract: Abstract. We identified, categorized, and discussed automatic defect inspection algorithms that analyze scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images for semiconductor manufacturing (SM). This is a topic of critical importance for the SM industry as the continuous shrinking of device patterns has led to increasing defectivity and a greater prevalence of higher-resolution imaging tools such as SEM. Among others, these aspects threaten to increase costs due to increased inspection time-to-solution and

papers.venue: Journal of Micro/Nanopatterning, Materials, and Metrology
papers.why_selected: Sections에서 SEM 기반 반도체 결함 검사 알고리즘의 체계적 리뷰를 제공하며, reference-based/no-reference 방법, 성능 메트릭(FP, IoU, AP), 처리 throughput, GPU 제약 등 본 연구의 평가 지표와 제약 조건을 포괄적으로 다룸.

papers.title: A Novel Method Based on Deep Convolutional Neural Networks for Wafer Semiconductor Surface Defect Inspection
papers.abstract: Semiconductor wafer is widely used in welding robot, spray robot, unmanned material delivery vehicle, and detection station sensor. The defects of semiconductor wafer, such as stains, burrs, scratches, and holes generated in the manufacturing process, severely affect the quality of downstream products. Therefore, the inspection of wafer defect could not be neglected. Traditional semiconductor wafer defect inspection methods based on handcrafted features heavily rely on the expertise and are limi
papers.venue: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
papers.why_selected: Deep CNN 기반 웨이퍼 표면 결함 검사 방법으로, 지도학습 기반 접근의 한계와 성능을 보여주는 비교 baseline 역할을 함.

papers.title: Towards improved semiconductor defect inspection for high-NA EUVL based on SEMI-SuperYOLO-NAS
papers.abstract: Continuous progression of Moore’s law brings new challenges in metrology and defect inspection. As the semiconductor industry embraces High-Numerical Aperture Extreme Ultraviolet Lithography (High-NA EUVL), there is a current industry-wide evaluation of this technology for potential pitch reduction in future nodes. One of the primary hurdles in implementing High-NA EUVL in High Volume Manufacturing (HVM) is its low depth of focus. Consequently, suppliers of resist materials are compelled to opt

sections.method: 3.1 Dataset 3.2 Proposed Data-augmentation strategy 3.3 Dataset preparation for training SR-assisted branch 3.4 Proposed SEMI-Super-YOLO-NAS architecture 3.5 Training methodology 4 Results and Discussion 4.1 Benchmarking proposed SEMI-SuperYOLO-NAS ADCD framework 4.2 Zero-shot inference with newly acquired High/Low resolution image pair (1024/512) 4.3 Inference with baseline YOLO-NAS on Super-Resolution images [Generated/Reconstructed with SR-assisted branch] 5 Limitations and Future Work
sections.limitation: License: arXiv.org perpetual non-exclusive license arXiv:2404.05862v1 [cs.CV] 08 Apr 2024 \authorinfo Further author information: (Send correspondence to Bappaditya Dey) Bappaditya Dey: E-mail: Bappaditya.Dey@imec.be Towards Improved Semiconductor Defect Inspection for high-NA EUVL based on SEMI-SuperYOLO-NAS Ying-Lin Chen Faculty of Science, KU Leuven, 3001 Leuven, Belgium Interuniversity Microelectronics Centre (imec), Kapeldreef 75, 3001 Leuven, Belgium Equal Contribution Jacob Deforce Interuniversity Microelectronics Centre (imec), Kapeldreef 75, 3001 Leuven, Belgium Faculty of Engineering, Ghent University, 9000 Ghent, Belgium Equal Contribution Vic De Ridder Interuniversity Microelectronics Centre (imec), Kapeldreef 75, 3001 Leuven, Belgium Faculty of Engineering, Ghent University, 9000 Ghent, Belgium Equal Contribution Bappaditya Dey Interuniversity Microelectronics Centre (imec), Kapeldreef 75, 3001 Leuven, Belgium Equal Contribution Victor Blanco Interuniversity Microelectronics Centre (imec), Kapeldreef 75, 3001 Leuven, Belgium Sandip Halder SCREEN SPE Germany GmbH, Ismaning, 85737, Germany This research was conducted during Sandip Halder’s tenure at imec Philippe Leray Interuniversity Microelectronics Centre (imec), Kapeldreef 75, 3001 Leuven, Belgium Abstract Continuous progression of Moore’s law brings new challenges in metrology and defect inspection. As the semiconductor industry embraces High-Numerical Aperture Extreme Ultraviolet Lithography (High-NA EUVL), there is a current industry-wide evaluation of this technology for potential pitch reduction in future nodes. One of the primary hurdles in implementing High-NA EUVL in High Volume Manufacturing (HVM) is its low depth of focus. Consequently, suppliers of resist materials are compelled to opt for thin resist and/or new underlayers/hardmask’s. Experimental combinations of thin resist materials with novel underlayers and hardmask’s seem to pose signal detection challenges due to poor Signal-to-Noise
sections.experiment: FAB datasets, as SEM-ADI and EDR-AEI. Furthermore, we demonstrate the ”Zero-Shot” inference capability by making predictions on defect classes not seen during training, utilizing ”(weakly) semantic embeddings.” In Section 2, we will review related research works on semiconductor defect inspection. Section 3 will delve into the details of the proposed methods and experiments conducted. Sections 4 and 5 will present our results and conclusions. 2 Related Work In this section, we review various works related to defect inspection in the semiconductor industry employing machine learning techniques. Chang et al. [ 7 ] proposed an unsupervised self-organizing neural network to address challenges in post-sawing wafer defect inspection, previously reliant on human inspection, thus requiring significant labor force and suffering from human fatigue. Dey et al. [ 8 ] utilized a ResNet architecture-based ensemble model for nano-scale defect inspection and classification. An unsupervised machine learning model was applied to reduce false-positive defects arising from SEM noise. Dey et al. also benchmarked another ensemble defect detection framework based on the state-of-the-art one-stage object detection model YOLOv5 [ 9 ] , which outperformed the previous ResNet-based ensemble framework. A data-centric approach was also demonstrated [ 10 ] , showing a way to consensually ensemble labels from different anonymous labelers for the same dataset, requiring minimal intervention from experts for defect labeling expectations. The final combined label, along with expert-aware post-processing data, yielded the highest test mAP of 0.919 with the state-of-the-art YOLOv8 model. The concept of pseudo-label prediction and model ensembles from different labelling partitions to avoid duplicate labeling work was proposed. Cheon et al. [ 11 ] utilized a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) along with a k-NN method to classify wafer surface defects from SEM images. The final activation layer outputs

papers.why_selected: Sections의 experiment에서 SEM-ADI/EDR-AEI 데이터셋에 대해 YOLO-NAS 기반 결함 탐지 성능(mAP 0.919)을 보고하고, zero-shot 추론 및 super-resolution 기반 접근을 다루어 반도체 SEM 결함 탐지의 supervised baseline 및 평가 기준을 제공함.

papers.title: Industrial Anomaly Detection with Domain Shift: A Real-world Dataset and Masked Multi-scale Reconstruction
papers.abstract: Industrial anomaly detection (IAD) is crucial for automating industrial quality inspection. The diversity of the datasets is the foundation for developing comprehensive IAD algorithms. Existing IAD datasets focus on the diversity of data categories, overlooking the diversity of domains within the same data category. In this paper, to bridge this gap, we propose the Aero-engine Blade Anomaly Detection (AeBAD) dataset, consisting of two sub-datasets: the single-blade dataset and the video anomaly
papers.why_selected: 도메인 시프트 하에서의 산업 이상 탐지 문제를 다루며 masked multi-scale reconstruction을 제안하여, 스타일/노이즈 변화에 강건한 anomaly detection 메커니즘의 근거를 제공함.

papers.title: Distance-Based Multivariate Anomaly Detection in Wire Arc Additive Manufacturing
papers.abstract: Wire Arc Additive Manufacturing (WAAM) offers the possibility to build up large-scale metal parts. Data which is obtained from a multivariate sensor system in-situ must be analyzed automatically to ensure an early and reliable detection of defects to reduce the costs due to production scrap. For that reason, a modular anomaly detector for multivariate time series in WAAM was investigated in this paper. The approach adressed major topics in real-life data sets of industrial applications such as m
papers.venue: International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications
papers.why_selected: 거리 기반 다변량 이상 탐지 방법을 WAAM 제조 공정에 적용하며, 산업 환경에서의 비지도 이상 탐지 일반 개념을 제공하지만 반도체 이미지 도메인과는 직접적 관련이 낮음.

papers.title: Unsupervised Anomaly Detection via Multi-Frequency Structure and Visual Sampling
papers.abstract: Unsupervised anomaly detection plays a critical role in industrial inspection, where anomalous samples are scarce and manual annotation is costly. However, detection performance often deteriorates in wild scenarios due to complex backgrounds and diverse anomaly appearances. To address the challenge, we propose a novel unsupervised image-level anomaly detection framework based on normalizing flows. First, a multi-frequency structural guidance module is introduced to separate irrelevant background
papers.venue: 2025 2nd International Conference on Image Processing, Multimedia Technology and Machine Learning (IPMML)
papers.why_selected: Normalizing flow 기반 비지도 anomaly detection에서 multi-frequency 구조 가이드와 visual sampling을 통해 복잡한 배경/다양한 이상 외관에 대한 강건성을 높이는 접근으로, 본 연구의 스타일 변화 강건성 메커니즘과 관련됨.

papers.title: Applications of Reference Cycle Building and K-shape Clustering for Anomaly Detection in the Semiconductor Manufacturing Process
papers.why_selected: 반도체 제조 공정에서 reference cycle 구축과 K-shape 클러스터링을 통한 이상 탐지를 다루어, reference 기반 비교 검사 개념의 반도체 도메인 적용 사례를 제공함.

papers.title: Deep Industrial Image Anomaly Detection: A Survey
papers.abstract: The recent rapid development of deep learning has laid a milestone in industrial image anomaly detection (IAD). In this paper, we provide a comprehensive review of deep learning-based image anomaly detection techniques, from the perspectives of neural network architectures, levels of supervision, loss functions, metrics and datasets. In addition, we extract the promising setting from industrial manufacturing and review the current IAD approaches under our proposed setting. Moreover, we highlight
papers.why_selected: 산업 이미지 이상 탐지의 딥러닝 기법을 신경망 구조, 지도 수준, 손실 함수, 메트릭, 데이터셋 관점에서 포괄적으로 리뷰하여 본 연구의 평가 기준 및 baseline 모델 선정의 참고 자료를 제공함.

papers.title: Contrastive Language Prompting to Ease False Positives in Medical Anomaly Detection
papers.abstract: A pre-trained visual-language model, contrastive language-image pre-training (CLIP), successfully accomplishes various downstream tasks with text prompts, such as finding images or localizing regions within the image. Despite CLIP's strong multi-modal data capabilities, it remains limited in specialized environments, such as medical applications. For this purpose, many CLIP variants-i.e., BioMedCLIP, and MedCLIP-SAMv2-have emerged, but false positives related to normal regions persist. Thus, we

sections.method: 2.2 Reconstruction-by-inpainting for UAD 3 Experiments 3.1 Experimental Setup 3.2 Qualitative results 3.3 Quantitative results
sections.limitation: 5 Acknowledgments Contrastive Language Prompting to Ease False Positives in Medical Anomaly Detection Abstract A pre-trained visual-language model, contrastive language-image pre-training (CLIP), successfully accomplishes various downstream tasks with text prompts, such as finding images or localizing regions within the image. Despite CLIP’s strong multi-modal data capabilities, it remains limited in specialized environments, such as medical applications. For this purpose, many CLIP variants—i.e., BioMedCLIP, and MedCLIP-SAMv2—have emerged, but false positives related to normal regions persist. Thus, we aim to present a simple yet important goal of reducing false positives in medical anomaly detection. We introduce a C ontrastive LA nguage P rompting ( CLAP ) method that leverages both positive and negative text prompts. This straightforward approach identifies potential lesion regions by visual attention to the positive prompts in the given image. To reduce false positives, we attenuate attention on normal regions using negative prompts. Extensive experiments with the BMAD dataset, including six biomedical benchmarks, demonstrate that CLAP method enhances anomaly detection performance. Our future plans include developing an automated fine prompting method for more practical usage. papers.why_selected: Sections에서 CLIP 기반 contrastive language prompting으로 정상 영역의 false positive를 줄이는 CLAP 방법을 제안하며, 본 연구의 false positive 억제 메커니즘에 대한 vision-language 기반 대안적 접근 근거를 제공함.

papers.title: Revisiting Reverse Distillation for Anomaly Detection
papers.abstract: Anomaly detection is an important application in large-scale industrial manufacturing. Recent methods for this task have demonstrated excellent accuracy but come with a latency trade-off. Memory based approaches with dominant performances like PatchCore or Coupled-hypersphere-based Feature Adaptation (CFA) require an external memory bank, which significantly lengthens the execution time. Another approach that employs Reversed Distillation (RD) can perform well while maintaining low latency. In t

papers.title: A Novel Dataset for Fabric Defect Detection: Bridging Gaps in Anomaly Detection
papers.abstract: Detecting anomalies in texture has become a significant concern across various industrial processes. One prevalent application of this is in inspecting patterned textures, especially in the domain of fabric defect detection, which is a commonly encountered scenario. This task entails dealing with a wide array of colours and textile varieties, spanning a broad spectrum of fabrics. Due to the extensive diversity in colours, textures, and defect characteristics, fabric defect detection presents a c

papers.title: InsPLAD: A Dataset and Benchmark for Power Line Asset Inspection in UAV Images
papers.abstract: ABSTRACT Power line maintenance and inspection are essential to avoid power supply interruptions, reducing its high social and financial impacts yearly. Automating power line visual inspections remains a relevant open problem for the industry due to the lack of public real-world datasets of power line components and their various defects to foster new research. This paper introduces InsPLAD, a Power Line Asset Inspection Dataset and Benchmark containing 10,607 high-resolution Unmanned Aerial Veh

papers.title: Unleashing Foundation Vision Models: Adaptive Transfer for Diverse Data-Limited Scientific Domains
papers.abstract: In the big data era, the computer vision field benefits from large-scale datasets such as LAION-2B, LAION-400M, and ImageNet-21K, Kinetics, on which popular models like the ViT and ConvNeXt series have been pre-trained, acquiring substantial knowledge. However, numerous downstream tasks in specialized and data-limited scientific domains continue to pose significant challenges. In this paper, we propose a novel Cluster Attention Adapter (CLAdapter), which refines and adapts the rich representatio

papers.title: Comparative evaluation of pretrained convolutional neural networks with attention enhancements for defect detection in injection-molded products
papers.venue: The International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology

papers.title: Emphasizing Discrepancies: A Hierarchical Discrepancy-Aware Framework for Industrial Anomaly Detection
Toward Efficient Network Traffic Classifications via Multimodal Learning
papers.abstract: With revolutionized various real-time and cyber-physical applications, today’s networked systems face more threats of cyberattacks due to their expansive networking attack surface. One promising trend is to deploy deep learning network (DNN) models to classify and identify cyberattacks at the network layer to identify either malicious traffic or anomaly traffic as alerts. However, deploying DNN models to classify network traffic will compromise the efficiency requirements of networked systems si

support.title: Scanning electron microscopy-based automatic defect inspection for semiconductor manufacturing: a systematic review
support.reason: Sections에서 SEM 기반 반도체 결함 검사 알고리즘의 체계적 리뷰를 제공하며, reference-based/no-reference 방법, 성능 메트릭(FP, IoU, AP), 처리 throughput, GPU 제약 등 본 연구의 평가 지표와 제약 조건을 포괄적으로 다룸.

support.title: Addressing Class Imbalance and Data Limitations in Advanced Node Semiconductor Defect Inspection: A Generative Approach for SEM Images
support.reason: Sections의 tables에서 DDPM 기반 합성 데이터로 YOLOv5/v8 학습 성능을 보고하며, 반도체 SEM 이미지의 class imbalance와 데이터 부족 문제를 다루는 지도학습 baseline으로 활용 가능함.

support.title: Attention Modules Improve Image-Level Anomaly Detection for Industrial Inspection: A DifferNet Case Study
support.reason: DifferNet 기반 비지도 이상 탐지에 attention 모듈을 추가하여 고해상도 산업 이미지에서 미세 결함 패턴을 감지하는 메커니즘을 제안하며, normal-only 학습 기반 anomaly detection의 baseline 역할을 할 수 있음.

contra.title: Achieving the Defect Transfer Detection of Semiconductor Wafer by a Novel Prototype Learning-Based Semantic Segmentation Network
contra.reason: Prototype learning 기반으로 다양한 배경 패턴 간 defect transfer detection을 수행하며, 배경 변화에 대한 강건성과 재학습 없이 새로운 패턴에 적응하는 문제를 다루어 본 연구의 style/noise 변화 강건성 요구사항과 직접 관련됨.

contra.title: Anomaly detection with convolutional neural networks for industrial surface inspection
contra.reason: CNN 기반 산업 표면 검사에서 정상 샘플만으로 anomaly detection을 수행하는 비지도 접근법을 다루어, normal-only reference 기반 결함 탐지 시스템의 핵심 선행 연구에 해당함.

contra.title: Machine learning based on using layout to generate reference SEM images for defect inspection
contra.reason: Layout에서 reference SEM 이미지를 생성하여 결함 검출에 활용하는 방법으로, 웨이퍼 상에서 직접 reference를 수집하지 않고 참조 이미지 기반 비교 검사를 수행하는 본 연구의 핵심 접근과 가장 유사함.

swing.title: Achieving the Defect Transfer Detection of Semiconductor Wafer by a Novel Prototype Learning-Based Semantic Segmentation Network
swing.gpt_reason: This paper is a key closest-prior-art contra signal directly challenging novelty and transfer robustness claims. If its claims are confirmed, then a prototype-learning semantic segmentation approach already addresses defect transfer across multiple wafer background patterns, weakening the hypothesis on key_innovation, expected_mechanism, and failure_modes. Because closest_prior_art is currently an uncovered required slot, confirming this paper would materially strengthen the case that the proposed contribution is not sufficiently novel or differentiated, pushing a borderline revise verdict toward reject.

swing.title: Anomaly detection with convolutional neural networks for industrial surface inspection
swing.gpt_reason: This paper is another strong closest-prior-art contra reference. If confirmed, it supports the view that CNN-based anomaly detection for industrial inspection already provides the relevant baseline mechanism and practical framing, reducing distinctiveness of the hypothesis on key_innovation, expected_mechanism, and baseline_models. Since closest_prior_art remains uncovered and this paper bears directly on that gap, confirmation would likely flip a tentative revise verdict to reject due to insufficient novelty over established industrial anomaly-detection methods.

swing.title: Machine learning based on using layout to generate reference SEM images for defect inspection
swing.gpt_reason: If confirmed, this paper shows that machine-learning generation of reference SEM images from layout already addresses a central semiconductor inspection need without requiring collected wafer reference images, directly competing with the hypothesis on key_innovation and expected_mechanism under realistic constraints. As a closest_prior_art paper in an uncovered must-cover slot, it could decisively undermine novelty and practical necessity, moving the verdict from revise to reject.
